name: Pushwoosh Push Notification API
what_it_does: Cross-platform push notification and customer messaging platform with REST API — delivering mobile push (iOS, Android), web push, in-app messages, email, and SMS from a unified API, with customer segmentation, automation workflows, A/B testing, and real-time analytics for mobile apps and websites.
use_cases: Sending targeted push notifications to iOS, Android, and web browsers via Pushwoosh API
Creating automated messaging workflows triggered by user behavior and events
Managing customer segments and sending personalized messages via Pushwoosh REST API
Integrating Pushwoosh with e-commerce platform for cart abandonment and order notifications
Pulling message delivery and engagement analytics for notification campaign reporting
not_for: Enterprise mobile marketing with advanced AI personalization (use Braze or Leanplum)
High-volume transactional SMS (use Twilio for transactional SMS at scale)
Complex cross-channel marketing orchestration (use Braze or CleverTap for enterprise)
best_when: A mid-market mobile app or website needs affordable, multi-platform push notification delivery with basic automation and segmentation — without enterprise marketing cloud costs.
avoid_when: You need sophisticated AI personalization, complex customer journey orchestration, or deep analytics beyond notification metrics.

auth.notes: API access token from Pushwoosh account settings. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. Webhook for delivery events (sent, delivered, opened). Mobile and web SDK for push registration. REST API for sending messages, managing segments, and querying analytics. No OAuth2. Application code required (different per app registered in Pushwoosh).

pricing.notes: Russian company (founded by Russian developers, now US entity). Founded 2011. Bootstrapped. Global customer base with privacy implications from Russian origins — evaluate data residency carefully. 15,000+ clients globally. Cross-platform push (iOS, Android, Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Samsung Internet). Email and SMS in addition to push. Competitive pricing vs OneSignal and Airship. Automation Campaigns for lifecycle messaging.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per plan; 1,000 requests/minute on Pro plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for push notifications, users, segments, and analytics. Bearer token auth. Good developer documentation. Application code required for each registered app. SDK for mobile and web push registration. Webhook for delivery events. Automation workflows configured in Pushwoosh UI. API can trigger individual and broadcast messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User profile reads idempotent. Message status reads idempotent. Message sends not idempotent — use request ID for deduplication tracking.
known_agent_gotchas: Russian-founded company — evaluate data residency and geopolitical risk for sensitive use cases
Application code (different per registered app) required in all API calls — not just API key
iOS push requires Apple APNS certificate or token — renew before expiry to avoid delivery failure
Web push requires browser permission — opt-in rates typically 5-20% for web push
Free tier limited to 1,000 subscribers — quickly outgrown for production apps

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR and CCPA compliant. US and EU data residency. API key auth. Russian-founded company — evaluate geopolitical risk for sensitive deployments; verify data residency SLAs. Mobile device tokens (APNS, FCM) are sensitive — store securely. User opt-in consent for push required in EU/UK under ePrivacy Directive. APNS/FCM credentials are highly sensitive — rotate if compromised.
